title: rosa sempervirens subsp. microphylla
intro: Rosa sempervirens subsp. microphylla, also known as Dwarf Evergreen Rose or Dwarf Wild Rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its small size and evergreen leaves. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The leaves and flowers of Rosa sempervirens subsp. microphylla can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and skin problems.
